title: Cookies, Credential Modes, API Keys, Bearer Tokens, and Secret Hygiene · Browser and Node Client Syste… · Compiler Case: Bearer Token Scope
summary: A browser client selects omit, same-origin, or include credentials and separates cookies, CSRF, API keys, and bearer-token authority. Planned artifact: Add and verify the http ts bearer token scope increment in a TypeScript 7 standards-based Fetch least-authority credential and session policy. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Acquire, attach, scope, refresh, expire, and revoke bearer tokens without treating possession as identity proof. Demonstrate the result through TypeScript 7 strict types plus WHATWG Fetch Request, Response, Headers, URL, streams, and AbortSignal contracts.

Place API keys in approved headers or signing inputs and exclude them from URLs, source, telemetry, and error text. Demonstrate the result through TypeScript 7 strict types plus WHATWG Fetch Request, Response, Headers, URL, streams, and AbortSignal contracts.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.
